source_paper_title: Analysis of the Primal-Dual Central Path for Nonlinear Semidefinite Optimization Without the Nondegeneracy Condition
context:
Section 4 (Concluding remarks and future work), page 19

There exist two directions for future works. The first one is concerned with limiting behavior of the tangential direction \(\dot x(\mu)\) in the \(x\)-space as \(\mu \to 0\). We have the following conjecture: \(\lim_{\mu\to 0}\dot x(\mu) = \xi^*\), where \(\xi^*\) was defined in Proposition 5.
title: Conjectured limit of the central path tangent \,\(\dot x(\mu)\) as \,\(\mu\to 0\) in nonlinear semidefinite programming
latex:
Given integers \(n,m,s\ge 1\), twice continuously differentiable functions \(f:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}\), \(G:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{S}^m\) (the space of real \(m\times m\) symmetric matrices), and \(h:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}^s\), consider the nonlinear semidefinite program
\[
\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^n}\ f(x) \quad \text{subject to}\quad G(x)\in\mathbb{S}^m_{+},\; h(x)=0.
\]
Let \(x^*\in\mathbb{R}^n\) be a KKT point, and let \((Y^a,z^a)\in\mathbb{S}^m_{+}\times\mathbb{R}^s\) denote the analytic-center Lagrange multiplier pair associated with \(x^*\) (i.e., the unique minimizer of \(-\log\det Y_{EE}\) over the Lagrange multiplier set \(\Lambda(x^*)\), with \(Y_{EE}\) defined via an orthogonal basis that diagonalizes \(G(x^*)\) and separates its nullspace).
Assume the hypotheses under which the paper proves existence of a unique smooth primal-dual central path (in particular: Mangasarian--Fromovitz constraint qualification at \(x^*\), strict complementarity at some multiplier matrix, and the enhanced second-order sufficient condition at \(x^*\)). Then there exist \(\bar\mu>0\) and a smooth mapping \(w(\mu)=(x(\mu),Y(\mu),z(\mu))\in\mathbb{R}^n\times\mathbb{S}^m_{++}\times\mathbb{R}^s\) for \(\mu\in(0,\bar\mu)\) such that \(w(\mu)\) satisfies the barrier KKT (BKKT) system
\[
\nabla f(x(\mu)) - JG(x(\mu))^*Y(\mu) + \nabla h(x(\mu))z(\mu)=0,\quad h(x(\mu))=0,\quad G(x(\mu))Y(\mu)=\mu I,
\]
with \(G(x(\mu))\in\mathbb{S}^m_{++}\) and \(Y(\mu)\in\mathbb{S}^m_{++}\), and \(\lim_{\mu\downarrow 0} w(\mu)=(x^*,Y^a,z^a)\).
Define \(\xi^*\in\mathbb{R}^n\) as the unique \(\Delta x\)-component of any solution \((\Delta x,\Delta Y)\in\mathbb{R}^n\times\mathbb{S}^m\) to the linear system
\[
U_{x^*}^\top\bigl(\nabla_{xx}^2 L(x^*,Y^a,z^a)\,\Delta x - JG(x^*)^*\Delta Y\bigr)=0,
\]
\[
G(x^*)\Delta Y + \Delta Y\,G(x^*) + Y^a\,\Delta G(x^*;\Delta x) + \Delta G(x^*;\Delta x)\,Y^a = 2I,
\]
\[
\nabla h(x^*)^\top \Delta x = 0.
\]
Here \(L(x,Y,z)= f(x) - G(x)\bullet Y + h(x)^\top z\), \(JG(x)^*\) is the adjoint Jacobian operator defined by \(JG(x)^*Y = (G_1(x)\bullet Y,\dots,G_n(x)\bullet Y)^\top\), \(\Delta G(x;d)=\sum_{i=1}^n d_i G_i(x)\), and \(U_{x^*}\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times p}\) has columns forming an orthonormal basis of \(\{d\in\mathbb{R}^n: \Delta G_{EE}(x^*;d)=0,\ \nabla h(x^*)^\top d=0\}\) (with \(\Delta G_{EE}\) denoting the \((EE)\)-block under the eigenbasis split of \(G(x^*)\)).

problem (conjecture): Prove or disprove that the tangent of the central path satisfies
\[
\lim_{\mu\downarrow 0} \dot x(\mu) = \xi^*,
\]
where \(\dot x(\mu)=\frac{d}{d\mu}x(\mu)\) and \(\xi^*\) is defined above.
